title: Ordering, Single Active Consumer, Keys, and Partitioning · Type-System Trace: Single Active Consumer
summary: A service promises global order across publishers and channels, adds parallel consumers, requeues failures, uses priority, then depends on exact FIFO completion for each customer. Planned artifact: Add and verify the single active consumer increment in ordering scope and partition strategy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use single active consumer when one active delivery stream is required and account for failover, prefetch, recovery, and throughput limits.
Partition related messages by stable business key while bounding skew, hot keys, resharding, and cross-key independence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Single Active Consumer · Theory Single Active Consumer
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 5081 in a privacy-sensitive audit fanout with an offline subscriber. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce partition, version, gap, and single-active evidence instead of claiming global order; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use single active consumer when one active delivery stream is required and account for failover, prefetch, recovery, and throughput limits.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Single Active Consumer contract for the supplied data.
